name: Remove Package
description: This script will remove a package in linux server
content:
---
- name: Completely Remove a Package from Linux Systems
  hosts: all
  become: yes
  gather_facts: yes

  vars:
    package_name: "nginx"  # Replace with the package you want to remove

  tasks:

    - name: Detect package manager
      ansible.builtin.set_fact:
        pkg_mgr: "{{ ansible_facts.pkg_mgr }}"

    - name: Check if package is installed
      ansible.builtin.shell: >
        {% if pkg_mgr == 'apt' %}
          dpkg -l | grep -w '^ii' | grep -w {{ package_name }} || echo 'not_installed'
        {% else %}
          rpm -q {{ package_name }} || echo 'not_installed'
        {% endif %}
      register: pkg_check
      changed_when: false

    - name: Show message if package is not installed
      ansible.builtin.debug:
        msg: "Package '{{ package_name }}' is not installed."
      when: "'not_installed' in pkg_check.stdout"

    - name: Stop, disable, and mask service if exists
      ansible.builtin.systemd:
        name: "{{ package_name }}"
        state: stopped
        enabled: no
      ignore_errors: yes
      when: "'not_installed' not in pkg_check.stdout"

    - name: Remove package completely
      ansible.builtin.shell: >
        {% if pkg_mgr == 'apt' %}
          apt-get purge -y {{ package_name }} {{ package_name }}* && apt-get autoremove -y
        {% else %}
          yum remove -y {{ package_name }}
        {% endif %}
      register: remove_result
      ignore_errors: yes
      when: "'not_installed' not in pkg_check.stdout"

    - name: Remove residual config directories
      ansible.builtin.file:
        path: "{{ item }}"
        state: absent
      loop:
        - "/etc/{{ package_name }}"
        - "/var/lib/{{ package_name }}"
        - "/var/log/{{ package_name }}"
      ignore_errors: yes
      when: "'not_installed' not in pkg_check.stdout"

    - name: Verify removal
      ansible.builtin.shell: >
        {% if pkg_mgr == 'apt' %}
          dpkg -l | grep -w '^ii' | grep -w {{ package_name }} || echo 'removed'
        {% else %}
          rpm -q {{ package_name }} || echo 'removed'
        {% endif %}
      register: verify_remove
      changed_when: false
      when: "'not_installed' not in pkg_check.stdout"

    - name: Show final removal result
      ansible.builtin.debug:
        msg: >-
          {% if 'not_installed' in pkg_check.stdout %}
            Package '{{ package_name }}' was not installed.
          {% elif 'removed' in verify_remove.stdout %}
            Package '{{ package_name }}' removed successfully.
          {% else %}
            Failed to completely remove package '{{ package_name }}'.
          {% endif %}

name: Update Package
description: This script will update the package in linux server
content:
---
- name: Update Package on Linux Systems
  hosts: all
  become: yes
  gather_facts: no

  vars:
    package_name: "nginx"  # Replace with your target package

  tasks:
    - name: Check if package is installed
      ansible.builtin.shell: |
        dpkg-query -W -f='${Package} ${Version}\n' {{ package_name }} 2>/dev/null || echo "not_installed"
      register: pkg_check
      changed_when: false

    - name: Show message if package is not installed
      ansible.builtin.debug:
        msg: "Package '{{ package_name }}' not installed."
      when: "'not_installed' in pkg_check.stdout"

    - name: Update the package to latest version
      ansible.builtin.apt:
        name: "{{ package_name }}"
        state: latest
        update_cache: yes
      when: "'not_installed' not in pkg_check.stdout"
      register: update_result

    - name: Get updated package version
      ansible.builtin.shell: |
        dpkg-query -W -f='${Version}\n' {{ package_name }}
      register: updated_version
      changed_when: false
      when: "'not_installed' not in pkg_check.stdout"

    - name: Show package update result
      ansible.builtin.debug:
        msg: >-
          {% if 'not_installed' in pkg_check.stdout %}
            Package '{{ package_name }}' not installed.
          {% elif update_result is defined and update_result.changed %}
            Package '{{ package_name }}' updated successfully to version {{ updated_version.stdout }}.
          {% else %}
            Package '{{ package_name }}' is already up-to-date (version {{ updated_version.stdout }}).
          {% endif %}

name: Install Package
description: This script will install a package in linux server
content:
---
- name: Manage Linux Service
  hosts: all
  become: yes
  gather_facts: no

  vars:
    service_name: "apache2"  # Replace with your target service

  tasks:

    - name: Check if service exists
      ansible.builtin.shell: "systemctl list-unit-files | grep -w {{ service_name }}.service || echo 'not_found'"
      register: service_check
      changed_when: false

    - name: Set service_not_found flag
      ansible.builtin.set_fact:
        service_not_found: true
      when: "'not_found' in service_check.stdout"

    - name: Set service_not_found to false if service exists
      ansible.builtin.set_fact:
        service_not_found: false
      when: "'not_found' not in service_check.stdout"

    - name: Show message if service does not exist
      ansible.builtin.debug:
        msg: "Service '{{ service_name }}' does not exist."
      when: service_not_found

    - name: Install the service package if not installed
      ansible.builtin.package:
        name: "{{ service_name }}"
        state: present
      when: service_not_found

    - name: Get installed service version
      ansible.builtin.shell: "dpkg -s {{ service_name }} | grep '^Version'"
      register: service_version
      changed_when: false
      when: service_not_found

    - name: Show message about successful installation and version
      ansible.builtin.debug:
        msg: "Service '{{ service_name }}' has been successfully installed with version: {{ service_version.stdout }}"
      when: service_not_found      

    - name: Check service full status
      ansible.builtin.shell: "systemctl status {{ service_name }}"
      register: service_status_full
      changed_when: false
      failed_when: false
      when: not service_not_found

    - name: Show service status based on systemctl output
      ansible.builtin.debug:
        msg: >-
          {% if 'Active: active (running)' in service_status_full.stdout %}
            Service '{{ service_name }}' is running
          {% elif 'Active: inactive (dead)' in service_status_full.stdout %}
            Service '{{ service_name }}' is stopped
          {% elif 'Active: failed' in service_status_full.stdout %}
            Service '{{ service_name }}' is failed
          {% elif 'Loaded: not-found' in service_status_full.stdout %}
            Service '{{ service_name }}' is not installed
          {% else %}
            Service '{{ service_name }}' status unknown
          {% endif %}
      when: not service_not_found

    - name: Install the service package if not installed (based on status)
      ansible.builtin.package:
        name: "{{ service_name }}"
        state: present
      when:
        - not service_not_found
        - "'Loaded: not-found' in service_status_full.stdout or 'could not be found' in service_status_full.stdout"

    - name: Log installation result
      ansible.builtin.debug:
        msg: "Service '{{ service_name }}' package has been installed."
      when:
        - not service_not_found
        - "'Loaded: not-found' in service_status_full.stdout or 'could not be found' in service_status_full.stdout"

    - name: Log that service is already installed
      ansible.builtin.debug:
        msg: "Service '{{ service_name }}' is already installed."
      when:
        - not service_not_found
        - "'Loaded: not-found' not in service_status_full.stdout"

name: Get Package Info
description: This script will provide you with the package info details in linux
content:
---
- name: Get Package Info
  hosts: all
  become: yes
  gather_facts: no

  vars:
    package_name: "nginx"  # Replace with your target package

  tasks:

    - name: Check if package is installed
      ansible.builtin.shell: |
        dpkg-query -W -f='${Package} ${Version} ${Status}\n' {{ package_name }} 2>/dev/null || echo "not_installed"
      register: pkg_check
      changed_when: false
      failed_when: false

    - name: Debug output for package check
      ansible.builtin.debug:
        msg: "Package check output: {{ pkg_check.stdout }}"

    - name: Set package_not_installed flag
      ansible.builtin.set_fact:
        package_not_installed: true
      when: "'not_installed' in pkg_check.stdout or 'not-installed' in pkg_check.stdout"

    - name: Ensure package_not_installed is false if package is present
      ansible.builtin.set_fact:
        package_not_installed: false
      when: "'not_installed' not in pkg_check.stdout and 'not-installed' not in pkg_check.stdout"

    - name: Show message if package is not installed
      ansible.builtin.debug:
        msg: "Package '{{ package_name }}' is not installed."
      when: package_not_installed

    - name: Get package details if installed
      ansible.builtin.shell: "dpkg-query -s {{ package_name }}"
      register: pkg_info
      when: not package_not_installed
      changed_when: false
      failed_when: false

    - name: Get package installation date
      ansible.builtin.shell: |
        stat -c '%y' /var/lib/dpkg/info/{{ package_name }}.list 2>/dev/null || echo "N/A"
      register: pkg_inst_date
      when: not package_not_installed
      changed_when: false

    - name: Check if package can be upgraded
      ansible.builtin.shell: |
        apt list --upgradable 2>/dev/null | grep -w {{ package_name }} || echo "no_upgrade"
      register: pkg_upgrade
      when: not package_not_installed
      changed_when: false
      failed_when: false

    - name: Display package details
      ansible.builtin.debug:
        msg: |
          {% if package_not_installed %}
            Package '{{ package_name }}' is not installed.
          {% else %}
            {% set desc_lines = pkg_info.stdout_lines | select('search','^Description:') | list %}
            {% set desc = desc_lines[0].split(':',1)[1] | default('N/A') | trim if desc_lines else 'N/A' %}
            Package Name: {{ package_name }}
            Version: {{ pkg_check.stdout.split()[1] }}
            Installation Date: {{ pkg_inst_date.stdout }}
            Description: {{ desc }}
            Upgradable to: {{ pkg_upgrade.stdout if 'no_upgrade' not in pkg_upgrade.stdout else 'N/A' }}
          {% endif %}

name: Service status/start/stop
description: Script to check status,, start & stop services in linux
content:
---
- name: Manage Linux Service
  hosts: all
  become: yes
  gather_facts: no

  vars:
    service_name: "apache2"  # Replace with your target service
    action: "status"          # Possible values: status/start/stop

  tasks:
    - name: Initialize service_state
      ansible.builtin.set_fact:
        service_state: not_installed

    - name: Check if service exists
      ansible.builtin.shell: "systemctl list-unit-files | grep -w {{ service_name }}.service || echo 'not_found'"
      register: service_exists
      changed_when: false
      failed_when: false

    - name: Check service status if exists
      ansible.builtin.shell: "systemctl show -p ActiveState {{ service_name }}.service --value"
      register: service_check
      changed_when: false
      failed_when: false
      when: "'not_found' not in service_exists.stdout"

    - name: Determine service status
      ansible.builtin.set_fact:
        service_state: >-
          {% if 'not_found' in service_exists.stdout %}
            not_installed
          {% elif service_check.stdout | trim == "active" %}
            active
          {% elif service_check.stdout | trim == "inactive" %}
            inactive
          {% elif service_check.stdout | trim == "failed" %}
            failed
          {% else %}
            unknown
          {% endif %}
      when: action == 'status'

    - name: Show service status
      ansible.builtin.debug:
        msg: >-
          {% set state = service_state | trim %}
          {% if state == "not_installed" %}
            Service '{{ service_name }}' is not installed on this server.
          {% elif state == "active" %}
            Service '{{ service_name }}' is running.
          {% elif state == "failed" %}
            Service '{{ service_name }}' is installed but in a FAILED state.
          {% elif state == "inactive" %}
            Service '{{ service_name }}' is installed but stopped.
          {% endif %}
      when: action == 'status'

    - name: Start the service
      ansible.builtin.systemd:
        name: "{{ service_name }}"
        state: started
      register: service_start
      when:
        - "'not_found' not in service_exists.stdout"
        - action == 'start'

    - name: Show service start result
      ansible.builtin.debug:
        msg: >-
          {% if service_start.changed %}
            Service '{{ service_name }}' started successfully.
          {% else %}
            Service '{{ service_name }}' was already running.
          {% endif %}
      when:
        - "'not_found' not in service_exists.stdout"
        - action == 'start'

    - name: Stop the service
      ansible.builtin.systemd:
        name: "{{ service_name }}"
        state: stopped
      register: service_stop
      when:
        - "'not_found' not in service_exists.stdout"
        - action == 'stop'

    - name: Show service stop result
      ansible.builtin.debug:
        msg: >-
          {% if service_stop.changed %}
            Service '{{ service_name }}' stopped successfully.
          {% else %}
            Service '{{ service_name }}' was already stopped.
          {% endif %}
      when:
        - "'not_found' not in service_exists.stdout"
        - action == 'stop'

name: Azure Linux VM Provisioning
description: Terraform Script Azure Linux VM Provisioning
